title: Artificial seawater medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eimug","text":"This artificial seawater medium was adapted from the Synthetic Crenarchaeota Medium described in Könneke et al. 2005 (10.1038/nature03911).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dissolve in 900 ml demineralized water: compound weight in g (for 900 ml medium) 1 NaCl 22 2 MgCl2 x 6 H2O 4.5 3 MgSO4 x 7 H2O 4.5 4 CaCl2 x 2 H2O 0.675
Autoclave at 121°C for 20 min at 15 psi.
After cooling to room temperature add (in the flow cabinet) from sterile stocks: compound amount (ml) 1 1M KH2PO4 0.261 2 TES I 0.45 3 TES II 0.9 4 Thauer’s vitamin solution 0.45 5 NaHCO3 1.8
Adjust the pH to 7.8 using sterile 1M NaHCO3 solution.

Dissolve in 1l demineralized water: compound weight in g (for 1 l) 1 Biotin 0.02 2 Folic Acid 0.02 3 Pyridoxine HCl 0.1 4 Thiamine HCl 0.05 5 Riboflavin 0.05 6 Nicotinic Acid 0.05 7 DL Pantothenic Acid 0.05 8 p Aminobenzoic Acid 0.05 9 Choline Chloride 2 10 Vitamin B12 0.01
Adjust to pH 7.0 with KOH.
Filter sterilize (0.2 µm) and store at 4ºC.
Dissolve 10 g NTA (Trisodium Nitrilotriacetate) in 500 ml demineralized water.
Filter sterilize (0.2 µm) or autoclave at 121°C for 20 min at 15 psi.
Store in the dark (slightly light sensitive) at RT.

Add the following ingredients and dissolve: compound weight in g (for 1 l) 1 ZnSO4 x 7 H2O 0.43 2 CoCl2 x 6 H2O 0.24 3 MnCl2 x 4 H2O 0.99 4 CuSO4 x 5 H2O 0.25 5 Na2MoO4 x 2 H2O 0.22 6 NiCl2 x 6 H2O 0.19 7 NaSeO4 0.1075 8 H3BO3 0.14 9 CeCl3 x 7 H2O 0.24
Filter sterilize (0.2 µm) or autoclave at 121°C for 20 min at 15 psi.
Store in the dark (slightly light sensitive) at RT.
Prepare the Thauer's vitamin solution and trace element solutions according to the recipes below. Aliquot for future use.
Adjust to pH 8 using NaOH pellets.
Add 5 g FeSO4 x 7 H2O and dissolve.
Top up with demineralized water to a total volume of 1l.
Top up with demineralized water to a total volume of 1l.
Adjust to pH 8 using NaOH pellets.
Dissolve 5.5 g NTA (Trisodium Nitrilotriacetate) in 500 ml demineralized water.
